title: trifolium badium subsp. rytidosemium
intro: Trifolium badium subsp. rytidosemium, also known as the "Himalayan Trifolium," is a subspecies of perennial herb in the pea family Fabaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The Himalayan Trifolium is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and powders, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
